---
title: "4.2.5 Integrated Development Environment (IDE)"
subtitle: "Software P170-P173"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)

## Integrated Development Environment

### A suite for writing and developing programs

An <span class="term">Integrated Development Environment (IDE)</span> (集成开发环境) is used by programmers to aid the writing and development of programs.

Some IDEs support one programming language. Others can be used for several different programming languages.

Examples in the textbook include PyCharm, Visual Studio and BlueJ.

## IDE Features

### Common tools

IDEs usually have these features:

::: {.tight-list}
- code editors
- a translator
- a runtime environment with a debugger
- error diagnostics
- auto-completion
- auto-correction
- an auto-documenter and prettyprinting
:::

## Code Editor

### Writing and editing programs

A <span class="term">code editor</span> allows a program to be written and edited without using a separate text editor.

This speeds up the program development process because editing can be done without changing to different software each time the program needs correcting or adding to.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.16 Visual Studio code editor screenshot placeholder
:::

## Translator

### Executing the program

Most IDEs usually provide a <span class="term">translator</span>, which can be a compiler and/or an interpreter, to enable the program to be executed.

The interpreter is often used for developing the program.

The compiler is often used to produce the final version of the program.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.17 Visual Studio code editor and program running screenshot placeholder
:::

## Runtime Environment

### Debugger

A <span class="term">debugger</span> runs the program under development and allows the programmer to:

::: {.tight-list}
- step through the program a line at a time
- set a <span class="term">breakpoint</span> to stop execution at a certain point
:::

A report window can show the contents of variables and expressions at that point in the program.

## Runtime Environment

### Checking logic

The debugger helps the programmer:

::: {.tight-list}
- see whether there are logic errors in the program
- check that the program works as intended
:::

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.18 PyCharm debugger screenshot placeholder
:::

## Error Diagnostics

### Dynamic error checking

<span class="term">Dynamic error checking</span> finds possible errors as the program code is being typed.

It can:

::: {.tight-list}
- alert the programmer at the time
- provide a suggested correction
- help find and correct errors before the program is run
:::

## Error Diagnostics

### Figure 4.19

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.19 Visual Studio error list with suggested corrections screenshot placeholder
:::

## Auto-Completion

### Context-sensitive prompts

Code editors can offer context-sensitive prompts with text completion for:

::: {.tight-list}
- variable names
- reserved words
:::

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.20 Visual Studio showing auto-completion screenshot placeholder
:::

## Auto-Documenter

### Explaining code

IDEs can provide an <span class="term">auto-documenter</span> to explain the function and purpose of programming code.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.21 Visual Studio auto-documenter screenshot placeholder
:::

## Prettyprinting

### Making code easier to read

Most code editors colour code the words in the program and lay out the program in a meaningful way.

This is called <span class="term">prettyprinting</span> (美化排版).

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.22 Visual Studio code editor showing prettyprinting screenshot placeholder
:::

## Activity 4.2

### Classroom practice prompts

Use the textbook activity to check understanding:

::: {.tight-list}
- decide whether translator statements are true or false
- describe three IDE features helpful when writing high-level language programs
- describe the difference between a compiler and an interpreter
- explain why a programmer would choose assembly language
:::

## Classroom Check

### Explain IDE features

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- an IDE helps programmers write and develop programs
- a code editor avoids using a separate text editor
- a translator lets the program be executed
- a debugger supports single stepping and breakpoints
- error diagnostics and auto-correction find errors while code is typed
- auto-completion, auto-documenter and prettyprinting help write and read code
:::

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)
